title: hibbertia glomerata subsp. wandoo
intro: Hibbertia glomerata subsp. wandoo, also known as Wandoo Guinea Flower, is a shrub-like plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Hibbertia glomerata subsp. wandoo has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory problems, with its leaves and flowers being used in herbal preparations. The plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
